revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Honjō Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Honjō Kofun cluster | native_name =本庄古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Honjo Kofun No28.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Honjō Kofun No.28 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Honjō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|31|59|20|N|131|19|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kunitomi, Miyazaki|en|Kunitomi, Miyazaki|qid=Q1350786|lt=Kunitomi, Miyazaki|1=Kunitomi, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 3rd to 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|59|20|N|131|19|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Honjō Kofun Cluster'''|本庄古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Honjō neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kunitomi, Miyazaki|en|Kunitomi, Miyazaki|qid=Q1350786|lt=Kunitomi|1=Kunitomi, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173495|title= 本庄古墳群|trans-title= Honjō Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Honjō Kofun Cluster is located on the Honjō Plateau at an elevation of about 40 meters, in an area extending approximately three kilometers from east-to-west and about one kilometer north-to-south, sandwiched between the Honjō River and Fukunen River, which are tributaries of the {{ill|Ōyodo River|en|Ōyodo River|qid=Q3594062|lt=Ōyodo River|1=Ōyodo River}}. These tumuli date from the late 4th century to the 6th century. When designated a National Historic Site in 1934 , the cluster consisted of 57 ''kofun'': 17 {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, 37 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tombs, two horizontal corridor cave-tombs, and one "vertical corridor [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]", which is a style unique to Kyushu. Kofun No. 37 is a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' with a total length of 73 meters, and the other keyhole-shaped tombs are also between 50 and 60 meters in length. Most keyhole-shaped tombs are built with the main axis oriented east-west, with the front facing west. Only cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been collected from Kofun No. 42, but the other tumuli have not been investigated. Some of the tumuli have local names, such as "Yoroizuka", "Kenzuka", and "Kutsuwazuka", indicating that at some point in the past, these Kofuns were robbed and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found within included such items as armor and iron swords. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per local legend, during the Kofun period this area was ruled by the Ushimori clan, who claimed descent from Prince Kunitomi, a grandson of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and these tumuli were claimed to be the graves of the successive clan chieftains. However, no [[archaeological excavation]]s have confirmed the full extent of the tombs.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
